---
title: "Linda Adade"
about:
  template: broadside
  
  image: profile.jpg.jpeg
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: Linkedin
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/linda-adade-194858205/
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/LindaAdade

---

I am a Master's student in Geography at the University of Oregon, where my research sits at the intersection of urban and economic geography, development, and China-Africa relations. I am interested in how investment, infrastructure, and economic processes shape places unevenly and what these patterns reveal about development and spatial inequality.

My current work examines the spatial distribution of Chinese-financed infrastructure across Africa and what it reveals about processes of uneven geographic development. I draw on GIS, spatial analysis, and mixed methods to investigate these questions.

Before joining the University of Oregon, I earned a Bachelor’s degree in Geography and Resource Development and Sociology from the University of Ghana, where my interest in human geography first took shape.

## Education

University of Oregon | Eugene, Oregon | MS in Geography | Sept 2025 - Present

University of Ghana | Accra, Ghana | B.A in Geography & Resource Development & Sociology | Sept 2016 - June 2020

## Experience

University of Oregon | Graduate Teaching Assistant | Sept 2025- Present

## Research Interests
- Economic Geography 
-	Urban Geography 
-	China-Africa relations
- GIS and Spatial Analysis 
-	Infrastructure and Regional Development
